summary: Discover custom-kind units at import from the active `temper.toml`'s declared `governs` loci, replacing the hardwired `specs/*.md` scan.

edit.description: `import::run` loads the project-root `temper.toml` layer (`compose::AuthorLayer::custom_kinds`, shipped) and scans, in addition to the built-in harness kinds, every custom kind it declares — each kind's `governs` locus discovered and its units projected to the surface. Replace the hardwired, unconditional `discover_spec_files`/`import_spec` call in `run`: spec discovery becomes data-driven like any custom kind; absent a `temper.toml` custom kind ⇒ built-ins only. Reconcile the inline `writes_a_spec_surface_and_rollup_row` test — it relies on the hardwired scan over a `temper.toml`-less fixture, so give its fixture a `temper.toml` declaring the spec kind (or fold it into the custom-kind path) plus a no-custom-kind case asserting built-ins only. Keep the `discover_spec_files` fn for now — drift's caller (`src/drift.rs`) still uses it; its removal is KIND-RETIRE-BUILTIN-SPEC.

tests.asserts: A harness with a `temper.toml` declaring a custom kind imports that kind's `governs` units to the surface via the data extractor; a harness with no declared custom kind imports built-in kinds only (no phantom `specs/` scan).

acceptance: `import` scans the built-in harness kinds plus every custom kind the active `temper.toml` declares (its `governs` locus), projecting each declared unit through the data extractor; the hardwired unconditional `specs/*.md` scan is gone.
notes: "temper reads its own `specs/` because its own `temper.toml` declares the `spec` kind, not because anything is hardwired" (`40-composition.md`). drift's `discover_spec_files` caller and the built-in `Spec` IR are retired in KIND-RETIRE-BUILTIN-SPEC once check also runs custom kinds via data.

summary: `check` dispatches each declared custom-kind artifact through its data extractor + contract; declare temper's own `spec` kind in a repo-root `temper.toml`.

per.section: Worked example: `spec`, temper's own custom kind

new.description: Declare temper's own `spec` custom kind via the mechanism: `governs = specs/*.md`, `[kind.spec.extraction]` (ATX headings, line count, placement, backtick-filename references), and a `[[kind.spec.clause]]` `max_lines` advisory (~150, per `90-spec-system.md`). The decisions-name-alternatives and references-resolve clauses are left out for now (see notes).

edit.description: In the `Check` arm, for each custom kind the active `temper.toml` declares, extract its artifacts via the declared data extraction and extend the diagnostic stream with `engine::admissibility` + `engine::validate` over the kind's contract — the same two greens the built-in kinds run.
Assert `temper check` over an imported corpus with a spec-declaring `temper.toml` runs the spec contract: over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity, exit 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.
Reconcile any end-to-end `check` assertion affected by a repo-root `temper.toml` now being present at the binary's cwd (the new spec kind participates).

tests.asserts: With a `temper.toml` declaring the `spec` custom kind, `temper check` extracts each spec via the declared extraction and runs admissibility + validate — over-length specs trip `max_lines` at advisory severity and the run exits 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.

acceptance: `temper check` over a corpus whose `temper.toml` declares the `spec` custom kind runs that kind's contract through the data extractor — flagging the over-length specs at advisory severity and exiting 0 absent `--deny-advisories`.
notes: Supersedes the dropped SPEC-KIND-GATE — no embedded `contracts/spec.toml`, the clause lives in `temper.toml`. Adding a repo-root `temper.toml` changes `temper check` cwd behavior for existing acceptance/cli runs, so those are in the blast radius. Only the `max_lines` clause ships; decisions-name-alternatives waits on `(decision-marker-predicate)`, references-resolve on KIND-EDGE-RELATIONSHIPS.

summary: Retire the built-in `spec` kind — `src/spec.rs`, `extract::spec_features`, and its hardwired import/workspace/drift plumbing — now the data-declared `spec` kind covers it.

per.section: Decision: a custom kind is declared data, never engine code

edit.description: Drop `spec_features` and its unit tests — a custom kind extracts via the data algebra, not a bespoke projector.
Drop the residual built-in spec plumbing (`import_spec`, any remaining `discover_spec_files` surface) not already removed by KIND-IMPORT-DISCOVERY.
Remove the `Workspace.specs` field and the built-in `Spec::from_surface_dir` load path (and its tests); custom-kind surface loading is generic, not spec-special.
Remove the spec drift axis that reads `workspace.specs` + `discover_spec_files`; reconcile drift to the generic custom-kind path.
Reconcile any end-to-end assertion referencing the built-in spec surface/IR.

tests.asserts: `temper` reads `specs/` solely through the data-declared custom kind — the built-in `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, and the spec-special import/workspace/drift plumbing are gone and the suite stays green.

acceptance: The built-in `spec` module and its bespoke plumbing (the `Spec` IR, `spec_features`, `import_spec`, `Workspace.specs`, drift's spec axis) are removed; `temper` reads `specs/` only through the data-declared custom kind; the whole suite is green.
notes: A custom kind shipped as a built-in breaks "temper ships none of them" — a stranger installing temper would inherit temper's `spec` kind. Cross-cutting: `Spec` is woven through import, check (`Workspace.specs`), drift (spec axis), extract, lib. Serialized last so specs stay discoverable + checkable via data throughout the chain; confirm no `check`/drift regression over `specs/`.

summary: Reconcile the top-level `[[edge]]` construct into `[kind.<name>.relationships]` so a reference is a kind capability, not a separate construct.

per.section: The harness is a graph too — and references are declared edges

edit.description: Move edge parsing from the standalone top-level `[[edge]]` array to `[kind.<name>.relationships]` under the owning kind — an edge is the source kind's declared reference field over its declared reference syntax. Preserve the parsed `Edge` shape the graph consumes.
Read the declared relationships off the owning kind rather than a free-standing edge list; route resolution + admissibility behavior unchanged.
Assemble the harness graph from the kinds' relationships instead of `AuthorLayer::edges`.
Update the edge-construction fixtures to the `[kind.<name>.relationships]` shape.

tests.asserts: Edges declared under `[kind.<name>.relationships]` assemble the harness graph and drive route resolution + admissibility exactly as the standalone `[[edge]]` construct did.

acceptance: A reference is declared under its owning kind's `[kind.<name>.relationships]` (over that kind's declared reference syntax) rather than a standalone `[[edge]]` table; the harness graph, route resolution, and edge-admissibility behavior are preserved.
notes: Note (d) of the reconciliation. The graph foundation shipped soundly (`src/graph.rs`, 54eb52f) over `AuthorLayer::edges`; this aligns the edge-declaration *shape* with the specced kind capability. `degree`/`acyclic` (the remaining graph-scope predicates) read the same relationships and follow as later entries; this also unlocks the spec kind's references-resolve clause (backtick-filename syntax).
